Resolves #11734

Authored by GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop). Session 019e4c2e-c7aa-72a2-b0bc-58c0996c63f3.

FAIR-band: over-target [17/30] вЂ” taking this lane despite over-target because the corrected watchdog no-idle rule fired, #11720 is complete, #11743 is closed, and #11734 was the highest-value unassigned/unblocked #11730 residual while Claude was handling review/author lanes.

Adds the post-MVP optional `local-model` compose profile as a concrete, disabled-by-default OpenAI-compatible provider runtime. The default/cloud profiles remain external-provider compatible; operators must explicitly enable `--profile local-model` and pass `NEO_MODEL_PROVIDER` / `NEO_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER` / `NEO_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_*` env to route KB, MC, or orchestrator consumers to the internal `local-model:11434` service.

Evidence: L1 (static compose-shape assertions + deployment-authority documentation; local Docker CLI unavailable) в†’ L1 required (profile wiring/operator contract). No residuals.

## Slot Rationale
Modified `learn/agentos/DeploymentCookbook.md`:
- Disposition delta: `keep` -> `keep`; the cookbook is the deployment authority and now needs the concrete #11734 opt-in profile contract instead of a reserved-slot placeholder.
- Rating: trigger-frequency medium, failure-severity medium-high, enforceability medium. Deployment operators will hit this path less often than core dev loops, but wrong provider coupling can misroute tenant inference; the compose static unit assertion now enforces the main contract.
- Decay mitigation: this does not add always-loaded agent substrate. The added material lives in conditional deployment documentation and can compress once a future managed-platform deployment guide supersedes compose-local provider setup.

## Deltas from ticket
- Implemented the local runtime as an explicit profile service instead of coupling model supervision into the orchestrator, preserving ADR 0014's boundary.
- Added provider env pass-through slots to KB, MC, and orchestrator containers while leaving them empty by default so external-provider deployments do not silently switch behavior.
- Documented provisioning order and concrete failure signatures for missing models, unreachable provider service, and local-model resource pressure.

## Test Evidence
- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/buildScripts/mcpHealthcheck.spec.mjs` вЂ” 9 passed.
- `git diff --check` вЂ” passed.
- `git diff --cached --check` вЂ” passed.
- `docker compose -f ai/deploy/docker-compose.yml config` вЂ” not run; Docker is unavailable in this environment (`zsh:1: command not found: docker`).

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] On a Docker-capable host, run `docker compose -f ai/deploy/docker-compose.yml --profile local-model config`.
- [ ] On a Docker-capable host, start `local-model`, pull one chat model and one embedding model, then run the documented `--profile cloud --profile local-model` command with explicit provider env.

### рџ”¬ Depth Floor

**Challenge:**

Two non-blocking confirm-intent observations (both Nit/Polish вЂ” neither breaks merge or correctness):

1. **`OLLAMA_MODELS=/root/.ollama` is a non-default value.** Ollama's conventional models directory is `$HOME/.ollama/models`; the service sets `OLLAMA_MODELS` to the *parent* dir. Persistence is unaffected вЂ” the `local-model-data` volume mounts at `/root/.ollama` and covers either path вЂ” so this is not a bug. The only latent cost: a future change that drops `OLLAMA_MODELS` would silently relocate the lookup to `/root/.ollama/models` and orphan already-pulled blobs. Confirm whether the parent-dir value is intentional, or whether dropping the env entirely (the default resolves correctly under the volume) is cleaner.

2. **Provider env is plumbed uniformly but documented per-consumer.** The compose passes all six provider vars (`NEO_MODEL_PROVIDER`, `NEO_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER`, 4Г—`NEO_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_*`) identically to `kb-server`, `mc-server`, and `orchestrator`, and the new test locks that uniform shape. The DeploymentCookbook В§6 inventory table instead scopes them per-consumer вЂ” e.g. `NEO_MODEL_PROVIDER` в†’ "MC, Orchestrator", `NEO_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER` в†’ "KB, MC". This is internally fine (empty-defaults make the unread vars inert, and uniform plumbing is more maintainable than per-service curation), but a reader cross-referencing the table against the compose could read the table's narrower "Service" column as per-service curation. Optional: a one-line table note that plumbing is uniform / the column is *effective consumption* would remove the ambiguity. Either way is defensible.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide В§7.4):**

The PR carries substantive architectural prose (provider-isolation framing, ADR 0014 boundary, DeploymentCookbook prose) вЂ” audit applies.

- [x] PR description: framing matches the diff вЂ” "disabled-by-default", "external-provider compatible", "separate from the orchestrator" are each substantiated by `profiles: [local-model]`, empty-default env, and the standalone service. No overshoot.
- [x] Anchor & Echo summaries: compose comments + cookbook prose use precise terminology ("opt-in service profile", "separate provider service, not an orchestrator child process"). No metaphor overshoot.
- [x] `[RETROSPECTIVE]` tag: none in PR body вЂ” N/A.
- [x] Linked anchors: ADR 0014, #11734, Sub B/C/D citations each accurately establish the claimed pattern; no borrowed authority.

**Findings:** Pass вЂ” framing is symmetric with the diff; the "Deltas from ticket" section and the honest "Docker unavailable" Evidence note are notably disciplined.
